title: Pungent Saudi Arabian Romano Pepper Tagine
description: A traditional Saudi Arabian dish featuring romano peppers in a mustardy and slightly sweet tagine, perfect for a comforting meal. This keto-friendly recipe is made with a soffritto base and has a unique glassy-jelly texture.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large clay or ceramic tagine or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize, stirring occasionally.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the ground cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the romano peppers and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the chicken broth and apple cider vinegar, and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes, or until the peppers are tender.
Remove the tagine from the heat and let it cool slightly.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro, if desired.
